title: Vehicle body builders and repairers
description: Perform engineering duties in designing, constructing, and testing aircraft, missiles, and spacecraft. May conduct basic and applied research to evaluate adaptability of materials and equipment to aircraft design and manufacture. May recommend improvements in testing equipment and techniques. Apply knowledge of engineering technology and biological science to agricultural problems concerned with power and machinery, electrification, structures, soil and water conservation, and processing of agricultural products. Design, develop, and evaluate the operation of marine vessels, ship machinery, and related equipment, such as power supply and propulsion systems. (Based on 31 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Explain proper operation of vehicle systems to customers.
Repair or replace parts, such as headlights, horns, handlebar controls, or mufflers.
Place wheels on balancing machines to determine counterweights required to balance wheels and mount them back onto vehicles.
Raise vehicles using hydraulic jacks or hoists.
Repair and replace tubeless tires by locating punctures, sealing punctures, and reassembling tires onto wheels.
Inspect tire casings for defects such as holes or tears and apply patches or adhesive as necessary.
Diagnose and repair furnace or air conditioning systems and inspect and repair vehicle heating and cooling systems.
Dismantle engines, inspect and repair or replace defective parts, and reassemble engines after repairs.
Inspect and repair brake systems, steering mechanisms, and align wheels using alignment equipment and balancing machines.
Conduct routine maintenance services, such as oil changes, replacing filters, and lubricating equipment and machinery.
Confer with customers, read work orders, and examine vehicles needing repair to determine the nature and extent of damage.
Remove damaged or broken exterior panels and install replacement parts using hand tools or welding equipment.
Test drive vehicles and diagnose malfunctions, ensuring repaired systems meet manufacturers' specifications.
Maintain cleanliness of work area and ensure proper functioning of tools and equipment used in repairs.
Document inspection and test results, as well as work performed or to be performed.
Use hand tools and power tools effectively for vehicle repairs, including wrenches, soldering irons, and pneumatic tools.
Dismount, mount, and repair or replace tires.
Repair or replace defective parts such as magnetos, water pumps, gears, pistons, and carburetors, using hand tools.
Inspect, repair, and maintain automotive and mechanical equipment and machinery, such as pumps and compressors.
Install, adjust, or repair hydraulic or electromagnetic automatic lift mechanisms used to raise and lower automobile windows, seats, and tops.
Diagnose and replace or repair engine management systems or related sensors for flexible fuel vehicles (FFVs) with ignition timing or fuel rate malfunctions.
Rebuild parts, such as crankshafts and cylinder blocks; or rebuild gas or diesel engines.
Conduct visual inspections of compressed natural gas fuel systems to identify issues.
Rewire ignition systems, lights, and instrument panels.
Install motorcycle accessories and fabricate or alter parts, using various hand tools.
Attach test instruments to equipment and read dials and gauges to diagnose malfunctions.
Inspect assembled vessels or individual components, such as tubes, fittings, valves, controls, or auxiliary mechanisms, to locate any defects.
Perform routine maintenance such as cleaning and oiling parts, honing cylinders, and tuning ignition systems.
Record repairs made, time spent, and parts used; and prepare cost estimates for repairs.
Remove engines from equipment, and position and bolt engines to repair stands or after maintenance.
Conduct pressure tests on vessels, such as boilers.
Evaluate performance of craft during dock and sea trials; and analyze data to determine feasibility of product proposals.
Prepare, or direct the preparation of, product or system layouts and detailed drawings and schematics.
Write technical reports or other documentation related to engineering projects, including handbooks or bulletins.
Prepare or present technical or project status reports and discuss plans with clients to evaluate and make necessary changes.
Validate design of fuel cell systems and recommend or implement changes to improve designs.
Simulate or model fuel cell and automotive system information using simulation software programs.
Establish production or quality control standards and coordinate activities with other functional units.
Design and analyze vehicle systems for improved performance, including control systems, safety, and alternative fuels.
Evaluate product data or design from inspections or reports for conformance to engineering principles and customer needs.
Conduct automotive design reviews, including calibration of systems and development of specifications for vehicle design.
Inspect samples and finished workpieces to ensure conformance with specifications using precision measuring devices.
Perform monitoring activities to ensure compliance with safety and environmental regulations for machinery and equipment.
Design complete hull and structures according to specifications, ensuring safety, efficiency, and economy standards.
Lift, position, and align fabricated parts to be welded or assembled according to blueprints and specifications.
Conduct research studies to develop new concepts in engineering to solve problems or improve designs.
Investigate equipment failures to diagnose problems and recommend remedial actions to improve functionality.
Place workpieces on cutting tables manually or using hoists, cranes, or sledges.
Measure and inspect machined parts to ensure conformance to product specifications.
Clean work areas and lubricate machines to maintain proper working conditions.
Operate forklifts to deliver materials and assist other workers with machine setup and removal of finished workpieces.
Refill, change, and monitor machine fluids, such as oil and coolant, and clean cutting tools using bench grinders.
Program computer numerical control machines and modify cutting programs to address operational issues.
Set up, operate, and tend machines to saw, cut, shear, or mill metal or plastic materials to specifications.
Study blueprints, work orders, and machine instructions to determine specifications and machining requirements.
Install, adjust, and remove machine tools and fixtures, ensuring proper alignment and selection of tools according to specifications.
Measure, inspect, and examine workpieces for defects and verify conformance to specifications using precision measuring instruments.
Start machines and monitor their operation, adjusting controls and settings to maintain product specifications and quality standards.
Select and set cutting speeds, feed rates, and depths of cuts, using knowledge of metal properties and machining techniques.
Lift, position, and secure workpieces using fasteners and hand tools for machining operations; manually or with hoists as necessary.
Troubleshoot and perform minor repairs on machines to ensure efficient operation and maintain production flow.
Record production output and monitor tool inventories and conditions to ensure adequate resources for operation.
Compute estimates and actual costs of factors such as materials, labor, or outside contractors.
Perform skilled repair or maintenance operations using equipment such as hand or power tools, hydraulic presses, or welding equipment.
Confer with personnel, such as management, engineering, quality control, or customer representatives, to coordinate work activities or resolve grievances.
Investigate accidents or injuries and prepare reports of findings.
Conduct or arrange for worker training in safety, repair, maintenance techniques, operational procedures, or equipment use.
Interpret specifications, blueprints, or job orders to construct templates and lay out reference points for workers.
Requisition materials and supplies such as tools, equipment, or replacement parts.
Meet with vendors or suppliers to discuss products used in repair work.
Monitor employees' work levels and review work performance.
Inspect and monitor work areas, examine tools and equipment, and provide employee safety training to prevent unsafe conditions or violations.

Repair or replace engine mechanical equipment, such as power tilts, water pumps, bilge pumps, or power take-offs.
